HOWEVER, IF THE DATE OF THE TRIAL OF THE COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED BY THE MASTER CALENDAR JUDGE TO A DAY AFTER JANUARY 1, 2019 THE TRIAL OF THE CROSS-COMPLAINTS WILL CEASE TO BE BIFURCATED AND THE NEW TRIAL DATE FOR THE TRIAL OF THE COMPLAINT WILL COVER THE TRIAL OF THE CROSS-COMPLAINTS AS WELL. REQUIRING KFHP TO PARTICIPATE IN A TRIAL IN THIS CALENDAR YEAR IS UNFAIRLY PREJUDICIAL TO KFHP. A TRIAL DATE ON THE CROSS-COMPLAINTS IN THIS CALENDAR YEAR WOULD REQUIRE KFHP TO FOREGO FILING A SUMMARY MOTION ASSERTING ITS CONTRACTUAL INTERPRETATION ARGUMENTS OR GIVE KFHP INSUFFICIENT TIME TO PREPARE SUCH A MOTION AS WELL AS PREPARE FOR TRIAL. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER OR MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) PARTIES TO EMAIL THE COURT RE: INFORMAL DISCOVERY. Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Cross-Complainant And Cross-Defendant Kaiser Foundation Health Plan, Inc. To Sever Cross-Claims From Personal Injury Action
Off Calendar Jul-12-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Khaldoun Baghdadi, Esq. 415-981-7210 of Walkup Melodia Kelly & Schoenberger for Plaintiff.
Marc Brainich, Esq. 510-267-3000 of Buty & Curliano for Defendant Kaiser Foundation Helath Plan.
Adam Polakoff, Esq. 415-398-3344 of Severson & Werson for Defendant Aguatierra.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

CROSS-COMPLAINANT AND CROSS DEFENDANT KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLAN, INC.'S Motion To Sever Cross-Claims From Personal Injury Action.

Cross-complainant and cross-defendant Kaiser Foundation Health Plan, Inc.'s motion to sever the cross-complaints from the complaint is treated as a motion for bifurcation of the cross-complaints and, so treated, is granted. The cross-complaints will be tried separately from the trial on the claims alleged in the complaint and the trial date for the trial on the cross-complaints will be determined by the civil master calendar judge or the judge who presides over the trial of the complaint after the trial of the complaint is concluded. However, if the date of the trial of the complaint is continued by the master calendar judge to a day after January 1, 2019 the trial of the cross-complaints will cease to be bifurcated and the new trial date for the trial of the complaint will cover the trial of the cross-complaints as well. Requiring KFHP to participate in a trial in this calendar year is unfairly prejudicial to KFHP. A trial date on the cross-complaints in this calendar year would require KFHP to forego filing a summary motion asserting its contractual interpretation arguments or give KFHP insufficient time to prepare such a motion as well as prepare for trial.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details) Parties to email the Court re: Informal Discovery.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-31-2018 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Justin Chou of the Law Offie of Walkup, Melodia Kelly & Schoenberger, Tel.No.
1-415-981-7210 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Marc Brainich of Buty & Curillano LLP, Tel. No. 1-510-267-3000 appearing for the
Defendant Kaiser Family Health;
Atty. Selene E. Plastiras of Severson & Werson, Tel. No. 1-415-398-3344 appearing for
the Defendant/Cross-Complainant Weiss Associates.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Cross-defendant Kaiser Foundation Health Plan, Inc.'s demurrer to all three causes of action in the cross-complaint of Aguatierra Associates, Inc. is overruled in its entirety. Kaiser's argument that the equitable indemnity claim is premature is unpersuasive. Even though Weiss has yet to pay any funds to Mr. Spingola, Weiss can bring a declaratory relief cause of action for equitable indemnity in this underlying tort action. (E.L. White, Inc. v. City of Huntington Beach, (1978) 21 Cal. 3d 497, 506; People ex rel. Dept. of Transportation v. Sup. Crt. (1980) 26 Cal.3d 744, 759-760; Valley Circle Estates v. VTN Consolidated, Inc. (1983) 33 Cal.3d 606, 612.) Kaiser's distinction between the equitable indemnity cause of action and the declaratory relief cause of action has little or no practical effect and both should proceed for the sake of judicial efficiency. This same reasoning applies to the contribution cause of action. The scope of the indemnification provision and whether its terms displace Weiss' equitable indemnity rights are not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. Paragraph 11.1 [indemnification] of the agreement, which the parties agree may be judicially noticed, refers to indemnity with respect to the asbestos work. Mr. Spingola's amended complaint and the cross-complaint do not allege claims involving asbestos work and extrinsic evidence is necessary to resolve whether that reference in the contract is a typographical error. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk
